channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kevin Koe advances to Brier final after extra-end win over Matt Dunstone
item.description: Alberta’s Kevin Koe is just one win away from claiming the Canadian men’s curling championship. In a tightly contested match in St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Koe’s Calgary-based team defeated...

item.title: House Fire Contained in Laurel Canyon Amid Strong Santa Ana Winds
item.description: On the morning of March 7, 2026, a three-story home located at 8522 West Oak Court in Laurel Canyon, California, was heavily damaged by a fire ignited on a balcony. The blaze, fueled by intense Santa ...

item.title: SERAP Takes Government to Court Over Unlawful Mass Phone Surveillance
item.description: The Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) don carry matter go ECOWAS Court of Justice against Nigerian government over the 2019 Lawful Interception of Communications Regulations. De...

item.title: Study Shows Telehealth Plays a Key Role in Care for Medicare-Enrolled Older Adults
item.description: A recent study published in the Annals of Internal Medicine highlights that Americans aged 65 and older with Medicare coverage logged approximately 60 million telehealth visits per year from 2021 to 2...

item.title: Middle East Airlines Resume Limited Flights Amid Regional Airspace Restrictions
item.description: Major airlines in the Middle East have started to cautiously resume flights after temporary airspace closures due to regional tensions related to the Iran conflict. Carriers like Emirates, Etihad Airw...

item.title: Trump Administration's Higher Education Compact Largely Rejected by Universities, but Revisions May Be Underway
item.description: In October 2025, the Trump administration proposed the Compact for Academic Excellence in Higher Education to nine major U.S. universities, later expanding it to over 100 institutions. The deal promis...

item.title: How to Prepare Eggs in Healthy Ways Without Risking Your Health
item.description: Eggs na one of di most nutritious food wey dey out there. Dem cheap, easy to cook, and packed with protein, healthy fats, vitamin B12, selenium, and choline. But if you dey cook dem wrong or pair dem ...

item.title: Remembering Soli Philander: Actor, Comedian and Advocate for Social Justice
item.description: Soli Philander, a cherished Cape Town actor, comedian and activist, passed away from cancer on 4 March 2026 at the age of 65. Known for his versatility and warmth, he worked across Afrikaans, English ...

item.title: National University and UE clinch the last Final 4 spots in UAAP men's tennis
item.description: In the ongoing UAAP Season 88 men's tennis competition, National University (NU) and the University of the East (UE) have officially completed the lineup for the Final 4 stage. With their recent victo...

item.title: Government Confirms Arrival of Three Petrol Shipments Amid Rising Fuel Prices
item.description: Petroleum Minister Ali Pervaiz Malik has informed that three petrol shipments are expected to arrive in Pakistan by tomorrow amid concerns over rising global oil prices and tensions in the Middle East...

item.title: Congress urges strict enforcement and review of air quality standards amid rising PM2.5 levels
item.description: On 8th March 2026, the Indian National Congress expressed strong concern over the deteriorating air quality across the country, urging the government to urgently review and upgrade the National Ambien...

item.title: Kenya's KSh35 Billion Flood Control Spending Fails Amid Recent Rains Due to Poor Planning
item.description: Kenya's government has spent KSh35 billion over the past four years on flood control projects, yet recent heavy rains have caused massive flooding, exposing serious flaws in planning and execution. Th...

item.title: NASA Observes Material Transfer Between Binary Asteroids
item.description: NASA's DART mission has provided the first direct visual evidence that asteroids in binary systems can exchange surface material. Using images captured before DART intentionally collided with Dimorpho...

item.title: Escalating U.S.-Israel-Iran Conflict Disrupts Travel for Canadians and Impacts Global Oil Production
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East have intensified as the U.S. and Israel continue military operations against Iran, now in its second week with no signs of de-escalation. U.S. President Donald Trump posted...

item.title: Middle East conflict escalates as Israel and US target Iran and regional attacks continue
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East has escalated significantly, with Israel and the United States conducting strikes on Iran following the killing of Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Israel...

item.title: Liquidity and Regulations Challenge Tanzania’s Microfinance Growth
item.description: Tanzania’s microfinance sector, which has been vital for small businesses, farmers, and low-income borrowers, is facing tough times. Many institutions are struggling with limited liquidity, making it ...

item.title: African Union urged to enhance measures against violence targeting women and girls
item.description: Violence against women and girls in Africa remains a severe and pressing challenge, with estimates from 2023 showing that one in three women aged 15 to 49 has experienced physical or sexual intimate p...

item.title: How Social Media Shapes Public Awareness During Crises
item.description: This article by Nadeem F. Paracha discusses the wide digital divide among social media platforms in Pakistan, especially during times of national crisis. He highlights how platforms like X (formerly T...

item.title: Opinions on Daylight Saving Time and Public Preferences for Light
item.description: The Globe and Mail's letters to the editor for March 8 include diverse perspectives on current Canadian issues, with a notable focus on daylight saving time. Readers expressed strong opinions on the n...

item.title: Discovery of an Isospin-Symmetric Island of Inversion in Molybdenum-84
item.description: Physicists have identified a surprising new 'Island of Inversion' in molybdenum-84, challenging long-held assumptions in nuclear physics. Traditionally, these regions, where atomic nuclei adopt highly...
